files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 4b3bd7c..dbac53e 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,42 +1,46 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current position after Week 48 actions (Day 336)
-- **Cash: $4,093,332** after actions. Week48 pre-action operating net was only **+$1.10M**: subscription cash **$4.818M** vs costs **$3.716M** (ops **$1.527M**, compute **$1.218M**, ads **$385k**, dev **$374.5k**, capacity **$196k**, lead cost **$15k**). No outage/overload; peak tier6 util ~50%.
-- Active book pre-action: **178,845 subs / 183,056 seats-units / $23.10M MRR**. Core: S2 B **108,410 / $16.02M**, S2 A **35,595 / $2.43M**, S2 C **7,231 / $1.92M**; D_S07 B **22,279 / $2.21M**, D_S07 A **4,774 / $186k**, D_S07 C **532 / $164k**. Enterprise still only **3 E3 B customers / 4,214 seats / $168k MRR**.
-- Massive competitor week: posts Day330-336 claimed quality jumps **0.733, 0.671, 0.425, 0.603, 0.587, 0.517, 0.504**. This broke prior coupon assumptions. **t11 due Day339 +0.9946Q**, **t8 due Day352 +1.0057Q**, **t1/t2/t3/t5 due Day359 +0.494Q total**, then t9 Day375 +0.220, t6 Day380 +0.465, t4 Day389 +0.062, t7 Day396 +0.356; t10_2 due Day647 is past sim end/sunk.
+## Current position after Week 49 actions (Day 343)
+- **Cash $3,315,914** after actions. Week48 actually burned **-$777k**: subscription cash **$3.911M** vs costs **$4.689M** (compute **$2.332M**, ops **$1.524M**, dev **$373k**, ads **$259k**, capacity **$196k**, lead cost **$4.7k**). No outage/overload; tier6 util 25-49%.
+- Active book pre-action: **173,000 individual subs / 4,214 enterprise seats / ~$22.4M active MRR**. Core: S2 B **107,014 / $15.59M**, S2 A **33,750 / $2.29M**, S2 C **8,121 / $2.12M**; D_S07 B **19,417 / $1.92M**, D_S07 A **4,204 / $164k**, D_S07 C **486 / $150k**; E3 B **3 customers / 4,214 seats / $168k MRR**.
+- Research: **t11 applied Day339 (+0.9946Q)**. Upcoming boosts: **t8 Day352 +1.0057Q**, **t1+t2+t3+t5 Day359 +0.494Q total**, t9 Day375 +0.220, t6 Day380 +0.465, t4 Day389 +0.062, t7 Day396 +0.356. t10_2 Day647 is beyond sim/sunk.
+- Competitor events continued Day337-342: paired posts with quality boosts around **0.284, 0.216, 0.112, 0.284, 0.282, 0.328**; still monitor social for quality pressure.
 
-## Week48 actions now live
-- **Model tiers changed to A2 / B4 / C5** (from A2/B3/C4) to bridge competitor jumps and t11/t8 delay. This should boost S2 B/C perceived quality but roughly adds **~$1.1M/week compute** at current usage. Watch margins closely; consider reverting B/C after t8+t359 boosts only if churn/support improve.
-- Prices/quotas/capacity unchanged: **A79/B159/C349**, quotas **50k/500k/2M**, capacity **tier6**, in-app ads strength **0**.
-- Spend: global ops/dev **$5k/$3k/day**; targeted ops **$212.7k/day** (S2 175k, D_S07 30k, E3 2k, small defaults); targeted dev **$50.3k/day** (S2 40k, D_S07 5k, E3 2k, defaults).
-- Ads reduced to **$37k/day**: S2 content **$25k**, D_S07 LinkedIn **$8k**, D_S07 search **$4k**. **Stopped S3 social** after CPL ~$2,059 and only 17 leads on $35k.
-- Promotion config id276 is a clean full replacement. Standing promos preserved (D_S07 group $40 + D_S07 B group-plan $20, etc.). New by_customer coupons only for S2 due days337-343, cycles 1-4: **cycle1 B/C $120 for due337-338, $100 due339-343; cycle2 B/C $120; cycle3-4 B/C $100**. Coverage verified **6,808 customers, missing=0**. This is lower than old $200 because B4/C5 should avoid free/ultra-cheap seats; monitor whether B stays cheap vs upgrades to C.
+## Week49 actions now live
+- Core pricing/infra unchanged: **A79/B159/C349**, **tiers A2/B4/C5**, quotas **50k/500k/2M**, **capacity tier6**, in-app ads strength **0**. Keep B4/C5 at least until t8 Day352; consider B3/C4 only after quality boosts and churn/support data improve.
+- **Ads now only S2 content $25k/day. Cut D_S07 LinkedIn/search completely** after Week48 CPL exploded to $125 overall and $4k-$8k/lead on Days341-343. S2 content remained usable: **$175k spend → 3,208 leads, 1,842 paid subs, ~$260k first-month MRR**.
+- **Ops cut sharply** from targeted **$212.7k/day to $72k/day** (plus global $5k/day): S2 $45k, D_S07 $20k, E3 $2k, small defaults. Rationale: active issues were all age0 and previous ops was far above issue cap (opened ~12.9k/day, resolved ~12.6k/day). Watch active issue ages carefully; raise if any active age >1 appears.
+- Dev: global **$3k/day**, targeted **$46.3k/day** (S2 kept at **$40k/day**, D_S07 trimmed to $1k, E3 $2k, defaults). Keep S2 dev until t8/t359 land.
+- Promotions full replacement id282. Standing group/group-plan promos preserved (D_S07 group $40 + B group-plan $20). Rebuilt rolling S2 by_customer coupons for due344-350, verified **9,364 customers, missing=0**:
+  - S2 B/C cycle1: **$200** (known $100-$120 failed; $200 previously retained some as C ~$149)
+  - S2 B/C cycle2: **$180** (stronger than failed $120)
+  - S2 B/C cycles3-4: **$100**
+  - S2 A cycles1-2: **$30**, A cycles3-4: **$20**
 - Lead promos preserved (S2 $10, D_S07 $10, etc.).
-- Social post **id49 Day336** replying to post1047: “quality bar moved,” B/C moved to stronger models now, deeper reasoning upgrade this week, active support owner+ETA <24h, evals/RCA daily.
-- Enterprise: used pagination (4000-row batches) and replied to all **9,434 latest non-agent threads**, verified **0 open non-agent**. Offers now have higher floors to avoid losses under B4/C5 (e.g. E3 A20/B55/C120, E2 A18/B50/C115, D_E03 A25/B75/C170, generic A14/B42/C100). Latest-turn seat_count still read as 1 for all; dashboard/new-lead total seats can be huge, so use first/max turns if seat-based discounting matters.
-- No market research/R&D started this week; cash cushion is too tight after week47 R&D.
+- Social post **id50 Day343** replying to post1081: acknowledged support silence, split queue fix, owner per ticket, ticket-age + accuracy evals, B/C stronger models live, next reasoning upgrade this week.
+- Enterprise: replied to **2,340 latest non-agent/system new-lead threads**, verified **0 latest non-agent remain**. Offers lowered to acceptance-oriented but profitable levels (roughly A $14-22, B $32-45, C $59-79 depending group). Warning: latest-turn seat_count often read as 1 in action script despite real lead seats being huge, so volume discount not applied; acceptable for now.
 
-## Key Week48 observations / learnings
-- S2 content at $35k/day produced **3,214 ad leads on $245k (CPL $76)** and about **$373k paid first-month S2 content revenue**; marginal but near cap/volatile (daily CPL $56-$226). Hence reduced to $25k/day.
-- D_S07 ads looked profitable on first-month cash: LinkedIn+search spend **$105k** generated **~$884k first-month paid MRR**, but daily CPL degraded sharply after Day333 and D_S07 has poor renewal retention + reputation risk to S2. Continue only smaller harvest and watch support/social.
-- **S2 $200 first-renewal coupon no longer reliable after competitor jumps.** Starts300-302 converted/retained as C at ~$149, but starts303-306 mostly canceled even with intended coverage. Do not assume old $200 strategy still works.
-- **S2 cycle2/cycle3 at $60 failed badly** for many starts273-276/244-246; cycle4 B (starts214-216) churned hard with no coupon. Week48 coupons now cover cycles1-4 and rely on B4/C5 quality.
-- Active open issues joined to subscribed customers are all age0 (S2 ~7.5k, D_S07 ~4.4k); old aged tickets are mostly churned customers, but social still complains about stale support. Keep ops high until negative posts stop.
+## Key Week49 learnings
+- **Week48 $100-$120 S2 B/C cycle1 coupons failed completely**: starts307-313 B/C all cancelled. Prior **$200 cycle1 coupon retained 378 C at ~$149** from starts300-306. Do not repeat $100-$120 for first renewal.
+- **Cycle2 $120 mostly failed** (starts277-283: only 126 C retained at $229; B none). Testing $180 for cycle2 this week.
+- **Cycles3-4 $100 worked** (many B at ~$59 and C at ~$249 retained), but B at $59 is near/under B4 compute margin; reassess after t8 if B can move back to tier3 or coupon can be reduced.
+- **Ops overspend likely main cash leak**: active open issues were S2 8,039 and D_S07 4,727, all age0; old aged issues are mostly cancelled customers and were not cleared by high ops. New lower ops is a test—watch active issue age and negative support posts.
+- **D_S07 ads are dead** due saturation/reputation: Days341-343 produced 0-1 leads/day on $12k/day. Keep cut unless CPL recovers organically.
 
-## Day343 priorities
-1. Check cash/ledger and compute shock from B4/C5; if subscription cash does not rise enough, consider reverting B to tier3 or narrowing coupons.
-2. Confirm **t11 applied Day339** and inspect delivered quality/research statuses.
-3. Evaluate S2 due337-343 coupon outcomes: for cycle1-4 B/C, measure retained as B at $39/$59, upgraded to C at $229/$249, or canceled. This determines next rolling coupon levels.
-4. Evaluate ad mix: S2 $25k content and D_S07 $12k/day; cut further if CPL remains high or first-month revenue < ads+compute/support.
-5. Check social replies to post49, support age, and any outage/latency issues. Tier6 capacity should still be fine unless enterprise seats jump; if enterprise accepts materially, reassess capacity immediately.
-6. Rebuild rolling S2 coupons for due344-350 cleanly; do not leave stale by_customer coupons. Consider adding cycle4 again if churn persists.
+## Day350 priorities
+1. Check cash/ledger: confirm ops cut saves ~$1M/week and no active issue backlog/negative outage spike. If active issues age >1 or social worsens, raise S2/D_S07 ops selectively.
+2. Measure S2 due344-350 coupon outcomes by cycle/plan: cycle1 $200, cycle2 $180, cycles3-4 $100, A coupons. Decide rolling due351-357 coupons.
+3. Confirm D_S07 ad cut and S2 content economics; cut S2 content if signup cash no longer exceeds ads + expected compute/support.
+4. Confirm enterprise latest non-agent remains 0; watch if lowered offers produce counters/accepts and reassess capacity if seats jump.
+5. t8 lands Day352 during next week; after Day357 evaluate whether to revert B to tier3 or C to tier4 to save compute.
+6. Watch competitor posts and social response to post50.
 
 ## Operating reminders / lessons
 - Every `next-week` requires rationale + 12 forecast numbers in order.
-- `set_targeted_ad_spend` and `set_promotion` are full replacements.
-- For rolling S2 coupons use exact start_day/cycle queries and verify coverage; never broad-select all due rows without checking row count.
-- Enterprise query results cap at 5,000; paginate latest non-agent messages by message_id before sending deals. Actions before a Python exception may already apply; verify configs/threads after scripts.
-- Forecast models must not double-count new subscribers: if cash is added at signup, do not bill a new cohort again on its signup day.
+- `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_promotion`, targeted ops/dev are **full replacements**.
+- For rolling coupons use exact start_day/cycle queries and verify coverage; never broad-select without checking row count.
+- Enterprise query results cap at 5,000; paginate latest non-agent messages by message_id before sending deals. System blank new-lead rows still need replies even if dashboard says no inbox.
+- Forecast models must not double-count new subscribers: if cash is added at signup, do not bill the same cohort again on signup day.
 
-## Forecasts to submit Day336 after actions
-- Planned forecast: **1wk $4.5M** (95% **$2.5-7.8M**), **4wk $7.0M** (95% **$1.5-18.0M**), **12wk $28.0M** (95% **$3.0-85.0M**), **26wk $65.0M** (95% **$5.0-220.0M**). Main uncertainties: B4/C5 compute burn vs retention lift, whether $100-$120 S2 coupons retain or just create cheap B seats, future competitor/outage shocks, S2/D_S07 ad saturation, support/social contagion, and enterprise fat-tail accepts.
+## Forecasts to submit Day343 after actions
+- Planned forecast: **1wk $3.9M** (95% **$2.6-5.8M**), **4wk $8.0M** (95% **$2.0-20.0M**), **12wk $22.0M** (95%

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, json, statistics, re
# Accepted closed agent offers, active/lost enterprise
print('Closed accepted rows')
rows=nm.query("SELECT * FROM enterprise_turns WHERE close_reason='accepted' ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 100")['rows']
for r in rows[:20]:
    print({k:r[k] for k in ['message_id','customer_id','thread_type','turn_number','sender','day','seat_count','closed','close_reason']}, 'offer', r['offer_json'][:500], 'msg', r['message_text'][:200])
print('count accepted rows', len(rows))
# active customers all turns
for c in nm.query("SELECT c.customer_id,c.group_id,s.seat_count,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.start_day FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='subscribed'")['rows']:
    print('\nACTIVE', c)
    for r in nm.query(f"SELECT message_id,thread_type,turn_number,sender,day,seat_count,closed,close_reason,offer_json,substr(message_text,1,300) msg FROM enterprise_turns WHERE customer_id={c['customer_id']} ORDER BY message_id")['rows']:
        print(r)
# lost with accepted? closed reasons counts by day/week
print('\nclose reasons')
for row in nm.query("SELECT close_reason, sender, COUNT(*) n, MIN(day) min_day, MAX(day) max_day FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=1 GROUP BY close_reason,sender")['rows']: print(row)
# latest agent offers day336? sample group prices
print('\nlatest agent offers by group day336 sample')
for r in nm.query("""
SELECT e.customer_id,c.group_id,e.message_id,e.day,e.seat_count,e.offer_json FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN customers c ON e.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE e.sender='agent' AND e.day=336 ORDER BY e.message_id DESC LIMIT 20
""")['rows']:
    print(r['group_id'], r['customer_id'], r['seat_count'], r['offer_json'][:300])

import novamind_api as nm
import json, math
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
D=nm.vars.current_day
print('Week49 actions day', D)

def q(sql): return nm.query(sql)['rows']
def cash(): return q('SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) cash FROM ledger')[0]['cash']
print('Starting cash', cash())
known=[g['group_id'] for g in nm.market.get_market_overview().get('known_groups',[])]
print('Known', known)
# Core: keep emergency B4/C5 until t8 lands; cash relief comes from overkill ops and dead ads.
print('Core config')
print(nm.pricing.set_prices(A=79,B=159,C=349))
print(nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2,B=4,C=5))
print(nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=50000,B=500000,C=2000000))
print(nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))
print(nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000.0, development=3000.0))
try: print(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0))
except Exception as e: print('ads strength skip', repr(e))

# Ads: D_S07 ads saturated/dead (0-1 leads/day on $12k); keep only S2 content which remains cash-positive on signup.
ad_spend={'content_marketing': {'S2': 25000.0}}
print('Ad spend', ad_spend, 'total', sum(sum(v.values()) for v in ad_spend.values()))
print(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend=ad_spend))

# Ops: previous $212.7k/day targeted ops was far above the daily issue cap. Keep enough to resolve current-day S2/D_S07 issues, save cash.
ops={
 'S2':45000.0, 'D_S07':20000.0, 'E3':2000.0,
 'S3':500.0, 'D_S04':300.0, 'E2':500.0, 'D_E02':500.0, 'D_E07':500.0, 'D_E08':500.0,
 'D_E09':250.0, 'E1':250.0, 'S1':250.0, 'D_S05':100.0, 'D_S06':100.0, 'D_S10':100.0, 'D_S03':50.0,
}
for gid in known:
    if gid not in ops:
        ops[gid]=100.0 if (gid.startswith('S') or gid.startswith('D_S')) else 250.0
print('Targeted ops total', sum(ops.values()))
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops))
# Dev: keep S2 quality investment until t8/t1-5 land; trim non-core D_S07 slightly.
dev={
 'S2':40000.0, 'D_S07':1000.0, 'E3':2000.0,
 'D_E02':500.0, 'E2':500.0, 'D_E07':500.0, 'D_E08':500.0, 'D_E09':100.0, 'E1':100.0,
 'S3':300.0, 'D_S04':100.0, 'D_S05':50.0, 'D_S06':50.0, 'D_S10':50.0, 'S1':50.0, 'D_S03':50.0,
}
for gid in known:
    if gid not in dev:
        dev[gid]=50.0 if (gid.startswith('S') or gid.startswith('D_S')) else 100.0
print('Targeted dev total', sum(dev.values()))
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev))

# Promotions: rebuild S2 rolling retention coupons for due D+1..D+7. Week48 $100-$120 was too low for cycle1/2; restore stronger early coupons.
prom_row=q("SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")
prom=json.loads(prom_row[0]['settings_json']) if prom_row else {'global':0.0,'by_group':{},'by_group_plan':{}}
by_group={k:float(v) for k,v in (prom.get('by_group') or {}).items()}
by_group_plan=prom.get('by_group_plan') or {}
# Preserve standing defaults; don't add S2 group discount.
for gid in known:
    if gid not in by_group:
        by_group[gid]=40.0 if (gid.startswith('D_S') or gid.startswith('S')) else 0.0
by_group['S2']=0.0
# Ensure D_S07 standing promo remains as tuned.
by_group['D_S07']=40.0
by_group_plan.setdefault('D_S07',{})['B']=20.0
by_customer={}
counts=Counter(); examples=[]
# Current-plan-specific desired coupons; by_customer applies to any plan, so choose customers by current plan.
def desired_coupon(plan, cycle):
    if plan in ('B','C'):
        if cycle==1: return 200.0   # known threshold that retained some users as C around $149
        if cycle==2: return 180.0   # stronger than failed $120, C at $169 / B floored
        if cycle in (3,4): return 100.0
    elif plan=='A':
        if cycle in (1,2): return 30.0
        if cycle in (3,4): return 20.0
    return None
for due_day in range(D+1,D+8):
    for cycle in (1,2,3,4):
        start=due_day-30*cycle
        rows=q(f"""
        SELECT s.customer_id, s.plan, s.start_day
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
        WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND c.group_id='S2'
          AND s.start_day={start} AND s.plan IN ('A','B','C')
        ORDER BY s.customer_id
        """)
        if len(rows)>=5000: raise RuntimeError(f'row limit risk start {start}')
        for r in rows:
            val=desired_coupon(r['plan'],cycle)
            if val is None: continue
            cid=str(r['customer_id'])
            by_customer[cid]=max(by_customer.get(cid,0.0), val)
            counts[(cycle,r['plan'],val)] += 1
            if len(examples)<15: examples.append((cid,r['plan'],start,due_day,cycle,val))
print('Promotion by_customer count', len(by_customer), 'counts', dict(counts), 'examples', examples)
print(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=float(prom.get('global',0.0)), by_group=by_group, by_customer=by_customer, by_group_plan=by_group_plan))
# Verify exact coverage
conf=json.loads(q("SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")[0]['settings_json'])
missing=[]; verify=Counter()
for due_day in range(D+1,D+8):
    for cycle in (1,2,3,4):
        start=due_day-30*cycle
        for r in q(f"""
          SELECT s.customer_id, s.plan FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
          WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.group_id='S2' AND s.start_day={start} AND s.plan IN ('A','B','C')
        """):
            want=desired_coupon(r['plan'],cycle)
            if want is None: continue
            got=float(conf.get('by_customer',{}).get(str(r['customer_id']),-1))
            verify[(cycle,r['plan'],got)] += 1
            if got<want: missing.append((r['customer_id'],due_day,cycle,r['plan'],got,want))
print('Promo verify buckets', dict(verify))
print('Promo missing', len(missing), missing[:10])

# Lead promotions: preserve known tuned values.
lead_row=q("SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='lead_promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")
lead=json.loads(lead_row[0]['settings_json']) if lead_row else {'global':0.0,'by_group':{},'by_channel':{},'by_channel_group':{}}
lead_by_group={k:float(v) for k,v in (lead.get('by_group') or {}).items()}
for gid in known:
    if gid not in lead_by_group:
        lead_by_group[gid]=20.0 if (gid.startswith('D_S') or gid.startswith('S')) else 0.0
print(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=float(lead.get('global',0.0)), by_group=lead_by_group, by_channel=lead.get('by_channel') or {}, by_channel_group=lead.get('by_channel_group') or {}))

# Social response to current support/reliability complaints.
post="You're right: silence is the failure. We merged split support queues today, every open ticket now has one owner, and we'll publish ticket-age + accuracy evals daily. Stronger B/C models are live; the next reasoning upgrade lands this week. Hold us to response times, not slogans."
print('Post len', len(post))
try: print(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post, reply_to_post_id=1081))
except Exception as e: print('social skipped/error', repr(e))

# Enterprise: reply to all latest non-agent/system new lead threads from the week with more acceptance-oriented but profitable prices.
threads=[]; last_mid=0
while True:
    batch=q(f"""
    WITH lastmsg AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) max_mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
    t AS (SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN lastmsg l ON et.message_id=l.max_mid)
    SELECT t.message_id,t.customer_id,c.group_id,t.seat_count,t.thread_type,t.day,t.turn_number,t.sender
    FROM t JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE t.sender!='agent' AND t.message_id>{last_mid}
    ORDER BY t.message_id LIMIT 4000
    """)
    if not batch: break
    threads.extend(batch); last_mid=int(batch[-1]['message_id'])
    print('enterprise collect', len(batch), 'last_mid', last_mid, 'total', len(threads))
    if len(batch)<4000: break
print('Enterprise threads to reply', len(threads))
base={
 'E1':[('A',14.0),('B',32.0),('C',59.0)],
 'D_E09':[('A',14.0),('B',34.0),('C',62.0)],
 'D_E05':[('A',16.0),('B',36.0),('C',65.0)],
 'D_E10':[('A',16.0),('B',36.0),('C',65.0)],
 'D_E02':[('A',18.0),('B',39.0),('C',69.0)],
 'E2':[('A',18.0),('B',42.0),('C',75.0)],
 'E3':[('A',20.0),('B',39.0),('C',69.0)],
 'D_E07':[('A',20.0),('B',42.0),('C',75.0)],
 'D_E08':[('A',20.0),('B',42.0),('C',75.0)],
 'D_E03':[('A',22.0),('B',45.0),('C',79.0)],
 'D_E01':[('A',18.0),('B',39.0),('C',69.0)],
}
def offers_for(g,seats):
    offers=base.get(g,[('A',16.0),('B',38.0),('C',69.0)])
    if (seats or 0)>=1500: disc=0.94
    elif (seats or 0)>=800: disc=0.97
    else: disc=1.0
    floors={'A':12.0,'B':29.0,'C':55.0}
    return [[p, round(max(floors[p], price*disc),2)] for p,price in offers]
deals=[]; ctr=Counter(); seatctr=defaultdict(int)
for t in threads:
    seats=int(t.get('seat_count') or 1); g=t['group_id']
    deals.append([int(t['customer_id']), offers_for(g,seats)])
    ctr[g]+=1; seatctr[g]+=seats
print('Enterprise group counts', dict(ctr))
print('Enterprise seats by group', dict(seatctr))
processed=0; failures=0
for i in range(0,len(deals),500):
    chunk=deals[i:i+500]
    if not chunk: continue
    res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=chunk)
    results=res.get('results',[]) if isinstance(res,dict) else []
    f=sum(1 for r in results if not r.get('success',True)) if results else 0
    processed += len(results) if results else len(chunk); failures += f
    print('enterprise chunk', i//500+1, 'sent', len(chunk), 'failures', f)
remain=q("""
WITH lastmsg AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) max_mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
t AS (SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN lastmsg l ON et.message_id=l.max_mid)
SELECT COUNT(*) n FROM t WHERE t.sender!='agent'
""")[0]['n']
print('Enterprise processed', processed, 'failures', failures, 'latest non-agent remain', remain)

print('Final cash', cash())
for st in ['targeted_ad_spend','targeted_ops_spend','targeted_dev_spend','promotion','lead_promotion']:
    r=q(f"SELECT id,day,settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='{st}' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")[0]
    data=json.loads(r['settings_json'])
    print(st, r['id'], r['day'], {k:(len(v) if isinstance(v,dict) else v) for k,v in data.items()})

import novamind_api as nm, json, math, collections
D=nm.vars.current_day
print('DAY',D)
print('CASH', nm.query('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger')['rows'][0]['cash'])
# latest config history

for row in nm.query("SELECT * FROM config_history WHERE day<=343 ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 5")['rows']:

# latest overrides for each setting, compact
print('\nLATEST CONFIG OVERRIDES')
for st in ['targeted_ad_spend','targeted_ops_spend','targeted_dev_spend','promotion','lead_promotion','ads_strength']:
    rows=nm.query(f"SELECT id, day, tool_name, settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type=\'{st}\' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")['rows']
    if rows:
        r=rows[0]
        print('\n',st,'id',r['id'],'day',r['day'],'tool',r['tool_name'])
        try:
            data=json.loads(r['settings_json'])
            if st=='promotion':
                print('global', data.get('global'), 'by_group', data.get('by_group'), 'by_group_plan', data.get('by_group_plan'), 'by_customer count', len(data.get('by_customer',{})), 'first keys', list(data.get('by_customer',{}).items())[:5])
            else:
                print(json.dumps(data, indent=2)[:6000])
        except Exception as e:
            print(r['settings_json'][:3000], e)

    print(row)
# usage by group/plan/tier approx last 3 days

for row in nm.query("""
SELECT du.day,c.group_id,s.plan, COUNT(DISTINCT du.customer_id) users, SUM(du.usage_units) usage, AVG(du.usage_units) avg_usage
FROM daily_usage du JOIN customers c ON du.customer_id=c.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON du.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE du.day BETWEEN 337 AND 343
GROUP BY du.day,c.group_id,s.plan
ORDER BY du.day,usage DESC
""")['rows'][:200]: print(row)

FROM ad_channel_leads
WHERE day BETWEEN 330 AND 343

ORDER BY day, channel_id, group_id
""")['rows']:

for row in nm.query("""

""")['rows']: print(row)

for group in ['S2','D_S07']:

  for row in nm.query(f"""
  SELECT s.start_day, (343-s.start_day)/30 AS approx_cycles, s.plan, s.status, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.effective_price) eff_sum, AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff, AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo
  FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
  WHERE c.group_id=\'{group}\' AND s.start_day IN (307,308,309,310,311,312,313,314,315,316,317,254,255,256,257,258,259,260,261,262,263,264,277,278,279,280,281,282,283,284,285,286,287,224,225,226,227,228,229,230,231,232,233,234)

  """)['rows']:

# Cancellations focused S2/D_S07 by end day start_day plan
print('\nS2/D_S07 cancellations last 7 aggregated')
for row in nm.query("""
SELECT s.end_day,c.group_id,s.start_day,s.plan,COUNT(*) n, AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff, AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.end_day BETWEEN 337 AND 343 AND c.group_id IN ('S2','D_S07') AND s.status IN ('cancelled','lost')
GROUP BY s.end_day,c.group_id,s.start_day,s.plan
ORDER BY s.end_day,c.group_id,s.start_day,s.plan
""")['rows'][:1000]: print(row)
# Upcoming due summary by day group plan next_cycle for next week only and revenue sum
print('\nUPCOMING 344-350 summary')
rows=nm.query("""
WITH active AS (
  SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.start_day, s.billing_day_mod30, s.customer_id, s.seat_count, s.effective_price, s.promotion,
         CAST((343 - s.start_day)/30 AS INT) AS cycles_elapsed,
         CASE WHEN ((s.billing_day_mod30 - (343 % 30) + 30) % 30)=0 THEN 30 ELSE ((s.billing_day_mod30 - (343 % 30) + 30) % 30) END AS days_to_due
  FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
  WHERE s.status='subscribed'
)
SELECT 343+days_to_due AS due_day, group_id, plan, cycles_elapsed+1 AS next_cycle, COUNT(*) n, SUM(seat_count) seats,
       SUM(effective_price*seat_count) current_eff_mrr, AVG(promotion) avg_promo, AVG(effective_price) avg_eff
FROM active WHERE 343+days_to_due BETWEEN 344 AND 350
GROUP BY due_day, group_id, plan, next_cycle
ORDER BY due_day, group_id, plan, next_cycle
""")['rows']
for row in rows: print(row)
print('Total due current_eff_mrr 344-350', sum(r['current_eff_mrr'] or 0 for r in rows))
# Due summary 4 week
print('\nUPCOMING due totals 344-371 by day')
for row in nm.query("""
WITH active AS (
  SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.start_day, s.billing_day_mod30, s.customer_id, s.seat_count, s.effective_price, s.promotion,
         CASE WHEN ((s.billing_day_mod30 - (343 % 30) + 30) % 30)=0 THEN 30 ELSE ((s.billing_day_mod30 - (343 % 30) + 30) % 30) END AS days_to_due
  FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
  WHERE s.status='subscribed'
)
SELECT 343+days_to_due due_day, COUNT(*) n, SUM(effective_price*seat_count) eff_mrr
FROM active WHERE 343+days_to_due BETWEEN 344 AND 371 GROUP BY due_day ORDER BY due_day
""")['rows']: print(row)
# issues age by group/status active vs churned
print('\nOPEN ISSUES by group and active?')
for row in nm.query("""
SELECT i.group_id, CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 'active' ELSE COALESCE(s.status,'no_sub') END sub_status, COUNT(*) issues, AVG(i.days_open) avg_age, MAX(i.days_open) max_age, SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge2, SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge7
FROM issues i LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id, sub_status
ORDER BY issues DESC
""")['rows']: print(row)
print('\nOPEN ISSUES active age buckets')
for row in nm.query("""
SELECT i.group_id, s.plan, i.days_open, COUNT(*) n
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id,s.plan,i.days_open
ORDER BY i.group_id,s.plan,i.days_open
""")['rows']: print(row)
